name: Barley

book_claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
book_claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine

book_claims.claim: Beta-glucan fibre in barley lowers glucose, insulin and cholesterol levels
book_claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan (found in appreciable amounts only in barley and oats) reduces intestinal absorption of cholesterol and glucose

supplementary_research.finding: A 2016 systematic review and meta-analysis (Am J Clin Nutr, PMID 27863994) of 14 RCTs found that barley beta-glucan consumption significantly reduced LDL cholesterol, non-HDL cholesterol, and apolipoprotein B, with a dose-response relationship starting at 3g/day.

A 2010 RCT (Br J Nutr, PMID 20142823) found that barley beta-glucan significantly reduced postprandial glucose and insulin responses in a dose-dependent manner, supporting the book's claims about glycemic control.

A 2015 systematic review (Eur J Clin Nutr, PMID 26269366) confirmed that barley-derived beta-glucan at 3g or more per day reduces total and LDL cholesterol, earning EFSA and FDA health claims for cholesterol reduction.

A 2016 BMJ meta-analysis of prospective studies (PMID 27301975) found that whole grain consumption including barley was associated with reduced risk of cardiovascular disease, cancer, and all-cause mortality in a dose-response relationship.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic pairing in krupnik and mushroom-barley soup
Acid brightens earthy grain in tabbouleh-style salads
Traditional hearty stew and soup base
Fat slows carbohydrate absorption and adds richness
Complementary amino acid profiles for complete protein
Mugi miso is traditionally made with barley koji

seasonality: Harvested late summer. Hulled barley stores well year-round. Available dried in bulk; look for hulled (not pearled) for maximum nutrition.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 2.5-11.3 g (varies by variety; hulled barley averages 5-8g)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Soluble fibre that forms a viscous gel in the gut, trapping bile acids and cholesterol. FDA-approved health claim at 3g/day for cholesterol reduction.
Mix of soluble and insoluble fiber. Among the highest fiber content of any whole grain.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Variable; some cultivars contain up to 10x more than brewer's yeast
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Trace mineral involved in insulin signaling and glucose metabolism.
Lost in pearling; hulled barley retains full thiamine content.
Cofactor for glutathione peroxidase antioxidant enzyme system.

synergies.description: Both tested together in cholesterol-lowering food portfolio trial; complementary soluble fibre types
Complementary amino acid profiles create complete protein when combined
